$comment: ELF64 EXECUTABLE (ET_EXEC) for AArch64 Linux. Ships at the D-LK6-1 sibling cycle (2026-06-01) — the closed-enum RelocFormulaKind reshape unblocked ARM64 reloc formula application; intra-module ET_EXEC works via the shared `applyExecRelocations` kernel. Consumed by `src/link/format/elf.cpp`. Extern imports + machine-specific PLT stub bytes (ARM64 ADRP+LDR+BR 16-byte sequence vs x86_64's 6-byte `FF 25 disp32`) landed at D-LK6-8 ✅ (`emitArm64PltStub` in `elf.cpp`); dynamic linking on ARM64 is exercised end-to-end by `examples/c-subset/extern_call_elf` (arm64 arm — an FFI `abs` call via the `direct-plt` dispatch → exit 42 on the native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` CI leg). Static self-contained execs with intra-module relocations also work.
$entryPointComment: Empty entryPoint defaults the walker to module.functions[0] (cycle-2 convention). Once D-LK1-1 closes (real symbol-name thread through HIR→LIR→AssembledFunction), this field will name the entry symbol and the walker will resolve it by name.
$externCallDispatchComment: ELF resolves imports through the PLT: the linker synthesizes a 16-byte ARM64 PLT stub per extern (ADRP+LDR+BR+NOP through the GOT slot) and points the extern symbol's VA at the STUB, so the call site is a PLAIN DIRECT call (BL imm26 to the stub, R_AARCH64_CALL26). No inline ADRP+LDR+BLR macro-op is needed at the call site -- the stub does the GOT indirection. D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS (rodata-only scope): the ELF ET_EXEC walker emits `.rodata` from `AssembledModule.dataItems` items with `section=Rodata`. The linker's pre-walker gate (linker.cpp) consults this set per-item — `Data` and `Bss` remain anchored under D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER. Same field + same shared `elf.cpp` code path as the x86_64-ELF exec format; arch differs only via config (pageAlign/machine). Cross-format agnosticism: zero format-name branches in the gate.
$processExitComment: STDIO-FLUSH-AT-EXIT (2026-06-09): terminate via libc `exit(3)`, NOT the raw `exit_group(2)` syscall. The C standard says returning from `main` ≡ calling `exit(3)`, which flushes stdio + runs `atexit` before the process dies; the prior raw `exit_group` SVC skipped that flush (a `puts("hello")`-then-return program exited cleanly but with stdout still buffered). By-name-import of libc `exit` makes the trampoline emit a DIRECT `BL` to the AArch64 PLT stub for `exit` (per externCallDispatch=direct-plt), exactly as a user FFI call lowers — so `exit` flushes, runs atexit, then issues the raw exit syscall itself. This routes EVERY AArch64-ELF program through the dynamic writer (libc.so.6 → DT_NEEDED, a 16-byte ARM64 PLT stub + GOT slot for `exit`, ld-linux-aarch64 resolves it) — the SAME machinery `extern_call_elf`'s arm64 arm already uses (D-LK6-8 ✅); the prior cycle's deferral of the GOT/PLT path for the exit mechanism is now realized via that proven dynamic-FFI substrate. importMangledName is the canonical C name `exit` (ELF adds no leading underscore). Uniform with macho (libSystem `exit`) + pe (kernel32 `ExitProcess`, msvcrt DLL-detach flush): all three terminate through a stdio-flushing libc-level primitive.
$entryCallingConventionComment: AAPCS64 calling convention — the user fn's return value (returnGprs[0] = x0) is moved into argGprs[0] (= x0) before SVC. Read via `target.callingConventionByName("aapcs64")` at trampoline-build time.

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity for an executable. type = ET_EXEC (2). class = ELFCLASS64; data = ELFDATA2LSB; osabi = sysv; machine = EM_AARCH64 (183). pageAlign = 4096 — most ARM64 Linux distributions ship with 4 KB pages. Apple Silicon (16 KB) and high-end ARM64 server configs (64 KB) declare their own values in their respective schemas; the same kernel PT_LOAD congruence rule (`p_vaddr % p_align == p_offset % p_align`) applies uniformly.
elf.$bindNowComment: Eager binding — same v1 stance as x86_64 ELF (plan 14 §5 risk row). Emits DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW so dyld resolves every GOT slot at load. Lazy upgrade anchored at D-LK6-11.

sections.$comment: Executable code section. virtualAddress = 0x400000 — ARM64 Linux convention (Linux loaders place ARM64 ET_EXEC at the same low base as x86_64 by default; AArch64 ELF psABI does not mandate a specific base). sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR (6); sh_addralign = 16 (cache-friendly; ARM64 instructions are 4-byte but section alignment is conventionally larger).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only initialized data (string literals, const/primitive globals). sh_type=SHT_PROGBITS(1); sh_flags=SHF_ALLOC(2) — readable, NOT writable/executable (it is folded into the R+X text PT_LOAD this cycle, which is strictly less permissive than writable; W^X preserved). sh_addralign=8. virtualAddress=0 — the writer COMPUTES the rodata VA contiguously after .text (secText.virtualAddress + alignUp(text.size, 8)); a fixed VA would collide with .text. D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS.
sections.name: .rodata

sections.$comment: Symbol table — conventional for debugger support. sh_type = SHT_SYMTAB (2); sh_addralign = 8; sh_entsize = 24 (Elf64_Sym).
sections.name: .symtab
